testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. Tests were pre-written and passed without modification.

criteria.criterion: AC1: koda kb search --project koda --query 'auth error' exits 0 and prints verdict + ranked results
criteria.details: Test verifies exit code 0 and output contains verdict, confidence, ranked results with ticket refs

criteria.criterion: AC2: koda kb search --json outputs raw JSON matching API response shape
criteria.details: Test verifies JSON output contains verdict, confidence, results array matching API response structure

criteria.criterion: AC3: koda kb list --project koda exits 0 and prints document table with ID, Source, Created columns
criteria.details: Test verifies exit code 0, table contains ID and Source columns, Created dates are rendered

criteria.criterion: AC4: koda kb add --project koda --file ./README.md exits 0 and prints success confirmation with file name and doc count
criteria.details: Test verifies exit code 0, output contains filename and document count

criteria.criterion: AC5: Missing --project or --query causes exit code 3 (manual validation)
criteria.details: All three commands validate required options manually and call process.exit(3)

criteria.criterion: AC6: Missing auth config (no apiKey or apiUrl) causes exit code 2
criteria.details: All three commands check auth and exit with code 2 when missing

criteria.criterion: AC7: API errors are handled via handleApiError(err)
criteria.details: All three commands wrap API calls in try/catch with handleApiError in catch block

criteria.criterion: AC8: kbCommand(program) is registered in apps/cli/src/index.ts after existing command registrations
criteria.details: kbCommand imported at line 13, registered at line 123 after projectCommand, ticketCommand, commentCommand, agentCommand, labelCommand

criteria.criterion: AC9: Unit tests in kb.spec.ts cover search verdict output, list table format, add confirmation, and auth-missing path
criteria.details: 32 comprehensive tests covering all scenarios: verdict/results output, table formatting, file read/upload, auth errors, validation errors, JSON output

criteria.criterion: AC10: bun run test passes in apps/cli

strengths: Follows established CLI patterns from agent.ts and other commands
Proper separation of concerns: validation → auth → API call → output formatting
Manual validation with explicit exit codes (3 for validation, 2 for auth, 0 success, 1 API error)
Consistent error handling using error() utility and handleApiError()
Supports both human-readable and JSON output with --json flag
scoreLabel() helper function cleanly separates score categorization logic
File reading uses readFileSync with basename for filename extraction

reasoning: All 32 tests pass successfully. Implementation meets all 10 acceptance criteria. Code quality is good: follows established CLI patterns, proper error handling with correct exit codes, supports both human and JSON output, and is properly registered after existing commands in index.ts. No test modifications were needed. The implementation is clean, maintainable, and consistent with the existing codebase conventions.
